items.title: Protocadherin γC4 regulates neuronal survival and dendritic self-avoidance
items.author: Takeshi Yagi
items.content: Two-step genome editing of protocadherin γC4 mutant mice highlights the essential role of full-length γC4 containing the cytoplasmic constant region in neuronal survival and Purkinje cell dendritic self-avoidance.

items.title: Rationally and in silico guided APOBEC3F-directed CBE for enhanced PDAC genetic therapy
items.author: Chao Yang
items.content: Evolutionary and structure-guided remodeling of APOBEC3F generates high-performance cytosine base editors with enhanced efficiency and accuracy, enabling robust KRAS and MYC silencing and therapeutic efficacy in pancreatic cancer models.

items.title: Addressing Vitamin B12 deficiency through aeroponic fortification of a salad crop (Pisum sativum)
items.author: Antony N. Dodd
items.content: A new and economically-feasible approach was developed for dietary supplementation with Vitamin B12 by a salad crop, through the use of aeroponic horticulture technology.

items.title: Early cAMP signaling orchestrates single-cell synchronicity throughout Dictyostelium development
items.author: Gad Shaulsky
items.content: Developmental synchronicity of individual Dictyostelium discoideum cells, measured with single-cell RNA sequencing of developing wild-type and mutant cells, reveals that early cAMP signals establish synchronicity throughout development.

items.title: ENA1 deficiency attenuates Saccharomyces ‘boulardii’ probiotic yeast virulence in immunosuppressed mouse fungaemia model
items.author: Walter P. Pfliegler
items.content: A new probiotic Saccharomyces ‘boulardii’ yeast strain with diminished virulence is developed using experimental infection and observations on in-host selection, achieved through the deletion of the ENA1 osmotic stress gene.

items.title: A multi-way SMILES-based hypergraph inference network for metabolic model reconstruction
items.author: Can Chen
items.content: Hypergraph-based modeling of metabolic networks integrates chemical structure and topology to accurately predict missing reactions, enabling efficient gap-filling and improved phenotype prediction in genome-scale metabolic models.

items.title: Nucleoli-localized KANSL2 as an epigenetic regulator of ribosome biogenesis in glioblastoma cells
items.author: Carolina Perez-Castro
items.content: Glioblastoma is a malignant brain tumor characterized by high cellular heterogeneity and an aggressive proliferative phenotype. This study identifies KANSL2 as a key epigenetic regulator of ribosome biogenesis in GBM cells.

items.title: Thioredoxin and its partner protein are essential for zoospore flagellar formation in Actinoplanes missouriensis
items.author: Yasuo Ohnishi
items.content: The actinomycete Actinoplanes missouriensis produces flagellated spores that become zoospores after being released from sporangia. A genetic study has revealed that a thioredoxin and its partner protein control flagellar assembly.
